title: Group Items by Calendar Month in Table View + Smarter Calendar Input
excerpt: I’d love to request the ability to group items by calendar month in the table view. This would be incredibly helpful for users like me who plan things like social media content where each item represents a post. I curren…

posts.author: Bailey_BMF (Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love to request the ability to group items by calendar month in the table view. This would be incredibly helpful for users like me who plan things like social media content where each item represents a post. I currently use the calendar view to visually schedule posts, which works great, but I still need to manually organize those items into monthly groups in the table — and right now, that means creating and selecting groups like “October,” “November,” etc., manually every time.

This becomes especially frustrating when I add an item from the calendar view — I click on a date to create a new post, but the item doesn’t automatically populate with that date. It would be so helpful if selecting a date in the calendar view pre-filled the date field on the new item.

Notion has a great calendar-based grouping system like this, and I’d love to see something similar in monday.com to make planning by month more seamless and reduce manual organization.

Thanks for considering this!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Bailey_BMF

The first part could be handled by creating a formula column to group by and using a formula like: FORMAT_DATE({Date}, "MMMM")

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: ebigley (Elise )
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree with you! I’m aiming to have blog posts grouped by brand with a separate view for the blogs grouped by month (based on due date) and cannot get this to work properly. Numerous other threads in this Community struggle to create this effectively.

posts.author: 0xm3t1 (Flynn .Is)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, one workaround is to use the Advanced Filter on your date column. Set it to “is between” and enter the first and last day of the month you’re targeting. It’s not automatic grouping, but it helps narrow your view by month pretty easily.

As for creating items from the Calendar View, the selected date should automatically populate the date field — even down to the time if you’re using the day view. Make sure that the calendar settings are correctly configured on your board; sometimes it’s just a quick tweak away.

Also, if you’re planning social media content, you might want to check out Publisher — it’s an app available in the monday Marketplace.

You can connect your social accounts, automate publishing to multiple platforms, auto-update item statuses, preview posts via a dedicated calendar, and even reuse content with post templates.

monday.com

Publisher : Social Media - monday.com Marketplace

Schedule & publish multichannel social media post templates.

Hope this helps!
